summaryText: Guest speaker Scott Williams, from Oklahoma City, delivered a dynamic message titled 'From Midnight to Daylight,' based on Acts 16. He opened by celebrating the work of God at Impact Church and the leadership of Pastors Travis and Natalie. Using the narrative of Paul and Silas's ministry journey, Pastor Scott illustrated how life can shift from seasons of breakthrough and success ('daylight') to sudden, difficult trials ('midnight'). He emphasized that God is present in both seasons and provided a three-part biblical framework for moving from spiritual darkness back into the light. The message encouraged believers to actively confront spiritual opposition, maintain worship in hardship, and intentionally lead others to Christ. Pastor Scott concluded with a powerful invitation for salvation, celebrating many who made decisions for Jesus, and encouraged everyone to read Acts 16 as homework to deepen their understanding of this transformative story.
mainPoints: Call out evil: Confront and reject spiritual opposition and sinful influences in your life, addressing the root problem and not just the person.
Call down Heaven: In your darkest 'midnight' moments, choose worship and prayer, trusting that if God brought you to a situation, He will see you through it.
Call up others: Live a life that points people to Jesus, using your testimony and influence to lead your household and others to salvation.

versesMentioned.text: Once when we were going to the place of prayer, we were met by a female slave who had a spirit by which she predicted the future. She earned a great deal of money for her owners by fortune-telling.
She followed Paul and the rest of us, shouting, "These men are servants of the Most High God, who are telling you the way to be saved."

She kept this up for many days. Finally Paul became so annoyed that he turned around and said to the spirit, "In the name of Jesus Christ I command you to come out of her!" At that moment the spirit left her.
The crowd joined in the attack against Paul and Silas, and the magistrates ordered them to be stripped and beaten with rods.
About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the other prisoners were listening to them.
Suddenly there was such a violent earthquake that the foundations of the prison were shaken. At once all the prison doors flew open, and everyone’s chains came loose.
He then brought them out and asked, "Sirs, what must I do to be saved?" They replied, "Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved—you and your household."
At that hour of the night the jailer took them and washed their wounds; then immediately he and all his household were baptized.

Monday.scripture: Acts 16:16-18 – "...Paul... said to the spirit, 'In the name of Jesus Christ I command you to come out of her!'"
Monday.action: Identify one negative influence or habit in your life. Pray specifically for God to break its power over you.
Monday.prayer: Lord, give me discernment to see the true source of my struggles and the courage to command it to leave in Jesus' name.

Tuesday.scripture: Acts 16:25 – "About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God..."
Tuesday.action: When you face a challenge today, pause and sing or listen to a worship song instead of complaining.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, help me choose praise over despair. Remind me that you are with me in my 'midnight' moments.

Wednesday.action: Reflect on a chain God has already broken in your life. Thank Him for that freedom and share it with one person.
Wednesday.prayer: Thank you, Jesus, for setting me free. Help me to live in that freedom and trust you to loosen every remaining chain.

Thursday.scripture: Acts 16:30-31 – "'Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved—you and your household.'"
Thursday.action: Pray by name for one family member or friend who does not know Jesus. Look for an opportunity to invite them to church or share your story.
Thursday.prayer: God, I lift up my household to you. Open doors for me to share the hope of salvation with them in a loving way.

Friday.scripture: Acts 16:33 – "...immediately he and all his household were baptized."
Friday.action: Is there a step of obedience (like baptism, reconciliation, or generosity) you've been delaying? Take one concrete step toward it today.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, highlight any area where I have delayed obedience. Give me the faith to act immediately, just as the jailer did.

Saturday.action: Fulfill the homework: Read all of Acts 16. Reflect on how the story moves from midnight to daylight and what it means for you.
Saturday.prayer: As I read your Word, God, show me where I am in the story. Renew my hope that daylight is coming and use me to call others up.
